headline: AGNICO EAGLE snaps up Finland's gold district in C$3.8B triple deal, uniting Kittilä and Ikkari
snippet: Agnico Eagle struck three simultaneous deals worth nearly C$3.8 billion — acquiring Rupert Resources (C$2.9B), Aurion Resources (~$350M), and B2Gold's 70% Fingold JV stake ($325M) — consolidating a 2,492 sq km land package in Finland's Central Lapland Greenstone Belt around its existing Kittilä mine, Europe's largest primary gold producer.

headline: USA RARE EARTH acquires $2.8B Brazil deposit, building first western mine-to-magnet rare earth chain
snippet: USA Rare Earth (Nasdaq: USAR) announced a $2.8 billion acquisition of Brazil's Serra Verde Group — the only producer outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at scale — sending the stock up 13.2% to $22.58. Serra Verde is expected to supply over 50% of ex-China heavy rare earth output by 2027, directly countering China's sweeping Announcement 18 export controls.

headline: GOLD slides to $4,763, silver drops 4% to $76.55 — bulls need a catalyst as Iran talks intensify
snippet: Gold pulled back to $4,763/oz and silver fell to $76.55 — down nearly 4% on the day — as markets assessed the US-Iran diplomatic landscape ahead of ceasefire expiry, with VP Vance expected to lead the US delegation. Gold remains $1,384 above its year-ago level; analysts are watching for a resolution catalyst to re-ignite the rally.

headline: CLSA lifts gold to $5,500 medium-term; SIL ETF breaks above multi-year resistance in one session
snippet: CLSA upgraded its gold price forecasts to $4,840 (2026), $5,130 (2027), and $5,500 (2028) as the metal's safe-haven bid holds. This week's Gold SWOT roundup also flags the silver miners ETF (SIL) surging 6.56% in a single session and decisively breaking above the key $38–$43 resistance zone — a technically significant move for silver equities.

headline: HIGH-GRADE BONANZA: IMPACT Silver hits 114.5 g/t gold, 1,295 g/t silver on Noche Buena extension
snippet: IMPACT Silver (TSX-V: IPT) reported fresh surface drilling results on the Carlos Pacheco Vein north extension at its producing Noche Buena mine in Mexico: 9.79 g/t Au and 212 g/t Ag over 9.49m, with a high-grade core of 114.5 g/t Au and 1,295 g/t Ag over 0.61m — extending the mineralized trend 150m beyond active mine workings. The company is evaluating copper as an additional target.

headline: Canada backs MANGROVE LITHIUM's next expansion — continental EV battery supply chain takes shape
snippet: The Government of Canada announced federal support for Mangrove Lithium's next development phase, days after the company opened North America's first commercial electrochemical lithium refinery in Delta, BC — a 1,000 tonne/year facility supporting roughly 25,000 EVs annually. The next phase targets an Eastern Canada plant capable of supplying 500,000 EVs per year.

headline: RECORD METAL PRICES fueling exploration budget surge, Heraeus says — juniors the next to benefit
snippet: German precious metals refiner Heraeus reports that gold's safe-haven status remains fully intact and that record-high gold and silver prices are translating directly into higher exploration budgets industry-wide — a lagging signal that has historically flowed through to junior exploration companies 12–18 months after a sustained metals bull market.

headline: MAGNA MINING posts best-ever quarter at McCreedy West: 5M CuEq payable lbs at 3.41% grade
snippet: Magna Mining (TSX-V: MGNA) released FY2025 results showing Q4 was the strongest quarter since acquiring McCreedy West — 5.0 million CuEq payable pounds at 3.41% CuEq from the 700 Footwall Copper Zone in Sudbury. The company closed 2025 with $55.9M cash and expects to complete feasibility studies on its Levack and Crean Hill projects by Q3 2026.

headline: Newmont Q1 EARNINGS THURSDAY — analysts forecast 65% EPS surge as gold averaged $4,934 in the quarter
snippet: Newmont (NYSE: NEM / TSX: NGT) reports Q1 2026 results on April 23. The consensus expects EPS of $2.07 — up 65.6% year-over-year — on revenues of $6.36 billion (+27%). Average realized gold in Q1 is estimated at $4,934/oz, but all-in sustaining costs are expected at $1,680/oz, above guidance, so cost discipline will be the key question on the call.
